summary:
The first permanent molar begins developing during fetal stage around week 20 but starts calcifying shortly before or right after birth; thus, it fits within one-year postnatal timing provided by option D. This process ensures proper mineralization necessary for functional teeth formation early childhood onward. 

The primary focus here was establishing when these crucial developmental milestones occur relative to each other—fetal vs neonatal/post-natal periods—to pinpoint accurate timeframes associated
